content:
# 2026-03-15

## Morning
- 6:30 tee time with Tom Hadley

## Done
- Walked the front nine
- Logged the round

## Open
- Call Judith about Zion dates

# 2026-03-14

## Morning
- Range session, worked the 7-iron

## Done
- Cleaned the clubs
- Booked next week's tee time

## Open
- Docent shift Thursday

# 2026-03-13

## Morning
- Coffee on the patio, watched the quail

## Done
- Read two chapters of the Doolittle Raid book
- Sorted the garage workbench

## Open
- Pick up dry cleaning

# 2026-03-12

## Morning
- Walked the loop with Judith before the heat

## Done
- Reviewed the museum docent rotation
- Logged the walk on Strava

## Open
- Confirm Thursday shift with Margaret

# 2026-03-11

## Morning
- Slow start, watched the sunrise

## Done
- Oil and filter on the 4Runner
- Called Jim in Tucson

## Open
- Start the Zion packing list

# Round — Troon North

Course: Troon North (Monument)
Played with: Tom Hadley, 6:30am

## Card
- Shot 84, handicap ~12
- Front 40, back 44

## Back nine fell apart
- Pushed tee shots right on 14 and 16
- Three-putted 18

# Handicap tracking

Current index: ~12

## Recent rounds
- Troon North 84
- TPC 86
- Grayhawk 82

## Notes
- Driving is steady; short game costs me 3-4 strokes a round

# Course strategy — Grayhawk (Raptor)

## Plan
- Keep driver in the bag on 4 and 12, hit 3-wood
- Favor left side into the par 5s

## Greens
- Fast and back-to-front; leave it below the hole
- Lag everything from above the pin

# Round — TPC Scottsdale (Stadium)

Course: TPC Scottsdale
Played with: Tom Hadley

## Card
- Shot 86, two over my handicap

## What went wrong
- Bunker trouble on 11 and 15
- Lost a ball in the desert off 17

# Zion packing list

## Bring
- Hiking boots, trekking poles
- Judith's binoculars and bird list
- Sun hats, 3L water each
- Permit printout for the Narrows

# Bryce Canyon checklist

## Before we go
- Reserve cabin near the rim
- Charge cameras
- Layers — cold at sunrise
- Map the Navajo Loop and Queen's Garden

# Grand Canyon — North Rim

## Notes
- Quieter than the South Rim, worth the drive
- Lodge books up early — reserve well ahead
- Judith wants the California condor viewpoints

# Trip planning

- Captured on 2026-03-15.
- Status: idea — pin down Zion dates with Judith.

# 4Runner checklist

- Captured on 2026-03-12.
- Status: idea — oil change and tires before the road trip.

# Courses to play

- Captured on 2026-03-15.
- Status: idea — try We-Ko-Pa next month.

# Round — Grayhawk Raptor

Course: Grayhawk (Raptor)
Played with: Tom Hadley, Walt Erikson

## Card
- Shot 82, season best

## What worked
- Kept driver in the bag on 4 and 12
- Lagged the fast greens below the hole

# Cardiology follow-up notes

## Dr. Kaplan — 6-month check
- No AFib recurrence since the 2024 cardioversion
- Stay the course on Eliquis and metoprolol

## Open
- Schedule the next visit; Judith drives

# Wine notes — February 2026

## Reds tried
- A Paso Robles GSM, good with the salmon
- A Rioja reserva Judith liked on the patio

## Note
- One glass with dinner; nothing fancy needed

# Museum exhibit ideas

## Thoughts
- Refresh the 737 cockpit panel placards
- Add a short walk-up briefing card for school groups
- Check with Margaret Chen on the docent handbook section

# SkyBridge alumni reunion

## Notes
- Tentative gathering for retired SkyBridge crew this winter
- Reserve a private room near Sky Harbor; easy for out-of-town folks
- Pull together a short slideshow from the 737 and 757 years
- Follow up with Walt about the guest list
